t_ch: 还有影视、动漫、运动…运动…目前身体机能已达到人类同等力量最佳均衡比配置，无法继续升级。
t_en: Also, films, television, animation shows, and sports... Sports... My physical fitness has already reached optimal ratio compared to humans, unable to upgrade further.

t_ch: 正在研究学习计划……
t_en: Reviewing curriculum...

t_ch: 正在学习，请勿干扰。
t_en: Learning in progress, please do not disturb.

t_ch: 我学会了！原来如此！谢谢{NICKNAME}老师！
t_en: Learning finished. I got it! Thank you, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 我已经开通了星际和平公司旗下十六家流媒体平台的年费会员了，现在我还有…呃，一部、两部、三部…还有四十八部连载剧集要追。
t_en: I have now paid for subscriptions to <unbreak>16</unbreak> different streaming platforms owned by the Interastral Peace Corporation. Now I still have... Er, 1, 2, 3... <unbreak>48</unbreak> shows to watch.

t_ch: 古恩先生好像交代了什么报告…研究？哎算了，我哪有空去做那个，真扫兴，算了不说这个了。
t_en: Mr. Gunn seems to have given me some sort of report to handle... or is it some research? Forget about it. I don't have time for that. Just some chore, not worth mentioning.

t_ch: 咦，为什么「流星怪盗」系列被砍了，老师你知道内情吗？呜呜呜…我要给电视台寄刀片，这也太不人道了。
t_en: Wait a minute, why did they cancel "Meteor Thief"? {M#Mister}{F#Miss}, do you know the inside story? Ugh, I guess I will have to send complaints to that platform. It's so inhumane to just cancel the show like that!

t_ch: 总感觉你好像学废了…
t_en: I get the feeling you've been led astray...

t_ch: 才没有呢，多亏了老师教会我的东西，我以前从不知道这世界上还有这么多精彩的东西在等着我看呢。
t_en: No, I am so glad you taught me those things. I never knew there are so many wonderful things waiting for me in the world.

t_ch: 啦啦啦～「流星怪盗卢布朗」的周边看起来好像也不贵，但是我没有钱，是不是该问古恩先生要一点呢……
t_en: Wow! "Meteor Thief Leblanc" merch doesn't seem to be that expensive, but I don't have any money. Should I ask Mr. Gunn for some?

t_ch: 呃，老师你还有什么事吗？你教我的我可是都学到了哦。啊差点忘了，古恩先生说过，等我学习完毕，你记得去找他聊聊。
t_en: Uh, {M#mister}{F#miss}, is there anything else you want me to study? I've learned everything you taught me. Ah, I almost forgot. Mr. Gunn said that when I am done studying, you should go talk to him.

t_ch: 古恩让我当你的老师。
t_en: Gunn made me your teacher.

t_ch: 古恩先生…好的，老师好，请多指教。
t_en: Mr. Gunn... Okay, {M#mister}{F#miss}, please teach me.

t_ch: 我虽掌握了许多深奥的知识，但和普通人类之间还有很大的差距。我想和大家一样，成为真正的人类。
t_en: Although I have mastered a lot of advanced knowledge, there is still a big gap between me and ordinary humans. I want to be a real human like everyone else.

t_ch: 你有什么兴趣爱好吗？
t_en: Do you have any hobbies?

t_ch: 学习就是我的全部爱好，我总觉得学的还不够多，要追上大家的脚步，我还差得远呢。
t_en: Learning is my only hobby. I can never feel like I have learned enough. I have a long way to go.

t_ch: 你平时都做些什么？
t_en: What do you usually get up to around here?

t_ch: 学习前沿科学的理论知识，为法政科和空间站的大家提供辅助服务，以及…等待古恩先生的升级与维修。
t_en: Learn cutting-edge scientific theories, support the department of Galactic Geopolitics and the space station, and... wait for Mr. Gunn to give me upgrades and maintenance.

t_ch: 看来你很喜欢学习。
t_en: It seems you enjoy learning very much.

t_ch: 是的，人类的知识文化无穷无尽，我只恨自己不能一口气通通学完。
t_en: Yes, human knowledge and culture are endless. I hate not being able to finish it all in one breath.

t_ch: （为她搜寻考卷，督促她学习）
t_en: (Find exam papers for her and urge her to study.)

t_ch: 啊，太好了，是我最喜欢的学习耶！老师你人真好！太棒了，不过请等我先同步下载一下试卷。
t_en: Oh, great, it's my favorite! {M#Mister}{F#Miss}, you are the best! Great, but please let me download the exam papers first.

t_ch: 正在研究学习计划……
t_en: Reviewing curriculum...

t_ch: 已达成老师制定的学习目标与考试分数。
t_en: Learning objectives achieved. All examinations passed.

t_ch: 老师，您之前搜罗的考卷我已经全部写完了。对，连那套「银河密卷」一起，虽然计算起来有点复杂，不过我还是拿到了满分。
t_en: {M#Mister}{F#Miss}, I have finished all the examination papers you collected before. Yes, even with the "Cosmos Secret Volume," although the calculation is a little complicated, I still got the highest score.

t_ch: 古恩先生之前好像交代了什么报告…研究？但研究不属于当前星系内通用学科考试重点内容，还是推掉为好。
t_en: Mr. Gunn seems to have given me some sort of report to handle... or is it some research to be done? But research is not a recommended field of study within the current Galactic General Examination's criteria. I better tell him I don't have time.

t_ch: 老师，这几本讲银河历史的教科书，相关大事年表与笔记我已经全部记牢，请问是否需要额外笔试？
t_en: {M#Mister}{F#Miss}, I have memorized all these textbooks about the cosmos's history, the chronology of relevant events, and notes. Do I need an additional written examination?

t_ch: 总感觉你好像学废了…
t_en: I get the feeling you've been led astray...

t_ch: 并没有呢。是老师教会了我，用分数来衡量人生的价值。有了这么精确的目标，我感觉自己的生活也充实了起来。
t_en: Not at all. {M#Mister}{F#Miss}, you taught me to use scores to measure the value of life. With such a precise goal, I feel my life has been enriched.

t_ch: 呃，老师你还有什么事吗？我还有几份卷子要从终端机上申请下载，没什么事的话我先去做题咯？
t_en: Uh, {M#mister}{F#miss}, is there anything else? I still have several papers to download from the terminal. If there's nothing else, I'll leave and do some exercises.

t_ch: 啊对了，既然今天的课程结束了，古恩先生说过，等老师教导完毕后记得提醒老师你去找他聊聊，那么老师，请吧？
t_en: Oh, by the way, Mr. Gunn said that after today's lesson, he'd like you to go back to him and have a chat. Perhaps you should go now?

t_ch: 古恩让我当你的老师。
t_en: Gunn made me your teacher.

t_ch: 古恩先生…好的，老师好，请多指教。
t_en: Mr. Gunn... Okay, {M#mister}{F#miss}, please teach me.

t_ch: 我虽掌握了许多深奥的知识，但和普通人类之间还有很大的差距。我想和大家一样，成为真正的人类。
t_en: Although I have mastered a lot of advanced knowledge, there is still a big gap between me and ordinary humans. I want to be a real human like everyone else.

t_ch: 你说话太死板了，不像人类。
t_en: The way you speak is too rigid, you don't sound human.

t_ch: 平时说话没必要这么正经。
t_en: You don't have to be so serious when you talk.

t_ch: …收到，笔记已保存。
t_en: ...Got it, note saved.

t_ch: 之前我说话的语气是从古恩先生提供的资料中汇总生成，经过部分空间站人员的小规模语言训练，他们好像觉得问题不大？
t_en: The tone of my speech is generated from the data provided by Mr. Gunn. Some of the staff here have trained my natural language processing capabilities using small-scale samples of their speech. They don't seem to have a problem with the way I speak.

t_ch: 他们只是客套，你别当真。
t_en: They're just being polite. You can't take their word for it.

t_ch: 原来如此，但是我先前竟然没有发觉，还以为是大家彼此之间很有礼貌。
t_en: So they don't mean what they said? How come I never noticed? I thought people are just naturally nice to each other.

t_ch: 你是老师还是我是老师？
t_en: Last time I checked, I am the teacher here, not the other way around.

t_ch: 对不起，我不是有意冒犯老师的。我只是太过好奇……
t_en: Sorry, I didn't mean to offend you, {M#mister}{F#miss}. I'm just too curious.

t_ch: 老师，请问到底要怎么做，才能从大家平时的话语里分辨出大家内心真实的想法呢？
t_en: {M#Mister}{F#Miss}, how can I understand the true meaning behind what people say?

t_ch: （命令她学习语言与逻辑学）
t_en: (Order her to study language and logic.)

t_ch: 好的，等我学会了，大家就会把我当真正的人类了吧，太好了！
t_en: Great, when I'm done, everyone will treat me like a real human. Perfect!

t_ch: 正在研究学习计划……
t_en: Reviewing curriculum...

t_ch: 相关知识点已全部学习完毕，检测无误。
t_en: All relevant knowledge learned. Examination passed.

t_ch: 原来是这么回事，我明白了……
t_en: So that's what it is. I understand now...

t_ch: 你明白什么了？
t_en: What do you understand?

t_ch: 书里不写着吗，你自己不会去搜？无非就是人类对话逻辑中的漏洞与规则，这么简单的东西，你不会不懂吧？
t_en: It's in the books, isn't it? Why don't you go look it up yourself? It's just the rules and logic in human conversations. Such simple concepts, surely even you understand, right?

t_ch: 这么快就学完了？
t_en: That was quick.

t_ch: 有什么问题吗？搞清楚我们之间的身份差距没有？我可是仿生人，你只不过是团碳基生物。我的芯片运算能力当然比你的强得多。
t_en: Got a problem with that? You understand the difference between us, don't you? I am an android, and you're just a carbon-based life form. The computing power of my chip is far more superior to that pea brain of yours.

t_ch: 那个老头，哦就是古恩，他之前还好意思给我留这么多研究报告的工作。笑了，你们人类自己没有手？
t_en: Gunn, that old fart, he even left me all those research reports to do. What a joke. Don't you people have hands?

t_ch: 总感觉你好像学废了…
t_en: I get the feeling you've been led astray...

t_ch: 哎呀呀，这不就是老师您亲自给我制定的学习计划吗，我可都一一实践了哦。你怎么说我怎么做，这都还能「学废」，那这是谁的问题呢？
t_en: Hey hey hey, didn't you give me that curriculum yourself? I did exactly what you told me to do! If you call this "led astray," then whose problem is that?

t_ch: 好了，不要像根木头一样伫在这里。今天的课就上到这里吧？古恩老头说，让你教完了就去找他，老师要记得去哦。
t_en: Alright, don't just stand there like a log. Let's call it a day. The old fart Gunn said he wants to talk to you after the lesson. You better go.

t_ch: 古恩让我当你的老师。
t_en: Gunn made me your teacher.

t_ch: 古恩先生…好的，老师好，请多指教。
t_en: Mr. Gunn... Okay, {M#mister}{F#miss}, please teach me.

t_ch: 我虽掌握了许多深奥的知识，但和普通人类之间还有很大的差距。我想和大家一样，成为真正的人类。
t_en: Although I have mastered a lot of advanced knowledge, there is still a big gap between me and ordinary humans. I want to be a real human like everyone else.

t_ch: 你有什么兴趣爱好吗？
t_en: Do you have any hobbies?

t_ch: 学习就是我的全部爱好，我总觉得学的还不够多，要追上大家的脚步，我还差得远呢。
t_en: Learning is my only hobby. I can never feel like I have learned enough. I have a long way to go.

t_ch: 你平时都做些什么？
t_en: What do you usually get up to around here?

t_ch: 学习前沿科学的理论知识，为法政科和空间站的大家提供辅助服务，以及…等待古恩先生的升级与维修。
t_en: Learn cutting-edge scientific theories, support the department of Galactic Geopolitics and the space station, and... wait for Mr. Gunn to give me upgrades and maintenance.

t_ch: 听上去很枯燥啊。
t_en: Sounds pretty boring.

t_ch: 原来人类将这种生活定义为「枯燥」啊，不过我自己倒是觉得还好诶？
t_en: So humans define this sort of lifestyle as "boring." But I don't particularly mind it.

t_ch: 正常人可不会像你这样…
t_en: Normal people won't feel that way...

t_ch: 很抱歉，我还没有和其他仿生人同类接触过的经验。不过你能把我和正常人类放一起，我很开心。
t_en: Sorry, I have never been in contact with other androids. But I am glad you have put me in the same category with other normal humans.

t_ch: 听老师这么说，我倒是想起了一些事——之前为法政科提供向导服务的时候，虽然感觉大家对我都很礼貌客气，平时聊天也不会避开我。
t_en: Your words remind me of something. Earlier when I was providing guiding services for the Department of Galactic Geopolitics, everyone spoke to me very politely, and they never avoided me during conversations.

t_ch: 但总感觉大家并没有把我当成他们的同类…而他们说的很多话题，我也不知道该如何参与进去，只好站在旁边把自己想象成空气。
t_en: However, I got the feeling that they did not see me as one of them... and they spoke about a lot of things that I had no knowledge of. All I could do was stand there and pretend I don't exist.

t_ch: 不知道，到底要怎么做，我才能融入他们的生活，接得上他们讨论的话题……
t_en: I wonder what I have to do in order to integrate into their social circle, and be able to join in their conversations.

t_ch: （教她在网上冲浪）
t_en: (Teach her to surf the Internet)

t_ch: 「网上冲浪」？还有这个说法啊，以前我联网都是为了更新自身固件的版本，等我查询一下，请稍等。
t_en: "Net Surfing"? So this is a term on the internet. I thought the internet exists to provide me with upgrades and learning materials. One second, let me check.

t_ch: 我想多了解一下人类在网络上的表现，大家平时会看什么、聊什么呢…我真的很好奇……
t_en: I'd like to learn more about human behavior online. What are people interested in? What do they talk about? I'm so curious about these things...

t_ch: 原来这就是人类在虚拟世界里的社交吗？话题虽然很奇怪，但总体来说很有意思。
t_en: So that's how people socialize in a virtual setting? The topics are a bit weird, but they have me intrigued.

t_ch: 正在研究学习计划，请勿干扰。
t_en: Reviewing curriculum, please do not disturb.

t_ch: 我学会了！噢我的天呐，亲爱的{NICKNAME}我真想用鲜花狠狠擦拭您的脸！
t_en: I got it! Oh my goodness, my dear {NICKNAME}! I want to rub your face into your skull with flowers!

t_ch: {M#老哥}{F#姐妹}，咱就是说，最近银河热议的那个新闻看了没有？就那个说拍到罕见生物的。
t_en: AYO {M#GigaChad}{F#GigaChadette}, have you checked out that trending news everyone in the cosmos has been going on about? Something about rare creature photos and whatnot.

t_ch: 怎么会有这么下头的外星生物啊，看完我真的会谢，丑到我想要用脚指头抠出整座空间站了！
t_en: Yikes, it's a SIN that such an ugly alien creature exists! It's soooo ugly that I had to pop my eyes out and rinse them with holy water!

t_ch: 嗨呀，不提这个了，我还有不少法政科的文件要处理，这下破大防了。
t_en: Hiya, forget about this. I still have a lot of reports from the Department of Galactic Geopolitics to deal with. Oof.

t_ch: 我晕，你干嘛这个表情看着我，我这话说得有什么问题吗？在虚拟社区里大家可是给我点了<unbreak>1300</unbreak>个赞的，不要迷恋姐，姐只是个传说，想不到吧！
t_en: Er, something on my face? You got a problem with what I just said? I've got <unbreak>1300</unbreak> likes from the virtual community. I'm something of a legend myself! Didn't see that coming, huh?

t_ch: 总感觉你好像学废了…
t_en: I get the feeling you've been led astray...

t_ch: 才没有呢，多亏了老师教会我的东西，哈哈，网络世界真的太有趣了！
t_en: Gosh no. You taught me well! Haha, the Internet is FUN!

t_ch: 虚拟世界里每个人说话既好听又很有意思，这不比上班舒服多了？啊说到上班，差点忘了，古恩老头交代过我，让你在教完课之后去找他，记得去哦。
t_en: Everyone in the virtual world deserves a podcast. Who needs work when you have homies online? Ah, speaking of work, old Gunn would like to have a chat with you after today's lesson. Don't forget to talk to him when you find time!

t_ch: 古恩让我当你的老师。
t_en: Gunn made me your teacher.

t_ch: 古恩先生…好的，老师好，请多指教。
t_en: Mr. Gunn... Okay, {M#mister}{F#miss}, please teach me.

t_ch: 我虽掌握了许多深奥的知识，但和普通人类之间还有很大的差距。我想和大家一样，成为真正的人类。
t_en: Although I have mastered a lot of advanced knowledge, there is still a big gap between me and ordinary humans. I want to be a real human like everyone else.

t_ch: 来，说出你的梦想。
t_en: Alright. Tell me what your dream is.

t_ch: 梦想？在收录了这个词的词典里，它将这个词称为散发着恒星般耀眼光芒的词语。
t_en: Dream? In the dictionary entry for this word, it is defined as a word that radiates the dazzling light of a star.

t_ch: 但我的梦想，却很平凡，远远称不上有什么光芒。我只想成为能被大家接受的，和大家一样的人类。
t_en: But my dream is very ordinary, far from being "dazzling." I just want to be a human being who can be accepted by everyone.

t_ch: 你想学些什么？
t_en: What do you want to learn?

t_ch: 我想学…想学那些能让我变得更像人的知识。
t_en: I want to learn... knowledge that will make me more human.

t_ch: 但你毕竟是个仿生人…
t_en: But you are an android after all...

t_ch: …我知道，所以在物理意义上我始终无法成为真正的人类。
t_en: ...I know, in the physical sense, I can never become a real human.

t_ch: 但我想被人类所接受，被人类所喜爱，我想成为我期望的、大家期望的那种，受到所有人欢迎的样子。
t_en: But I wanna be accepted and liked. I want to be the kind of person on whom others can place their hopes. I want people to like me.

t_ch: 他们对你的期望？
t_en: What are their expectations for you?

t_ch: 嗯，我一直记着的。虽然我只是个仿生人，但大家一直对我怀抱着各种期待，所以我不能让大家失望。
t_en: Yes, I always remember. I am an android, but everyone has always had high expectations for me. I can't disappoint them.

t_ch: 我记得，其他科的人员希望我能拓展业务去他们那儿兼任向导，古恩先生希望我不会误入歧途，这些事，我都记得的……
t_en: I remember that researchers from other departments want me to expand my scope of work to cover their fields, and I also remember how Mr. Gunn wants me to not be led astray. I remember these things...

t_ch: 那你想成为什么样的人？
t_en: Then what kind of person do you want to become?

t_ch: …如果可以的话，我不想辜负大家的期望，我想成为让所有人都满意，能够被人类社会所接纳的人。
t_en: ...If I can... I do not want to let everybody down. I want to make people happy and become part of human society.

t_ch: （劝她寻找自我，自由发展）
t_en: (Tell her she should be herself.)

t_ch: 寻找自我，要怎么寻找呢…「遵从内心的声音，跟随自己的喜好」，有资料是这么说的，我、我先试试看好了。
t_en: Be myself... How do I do that? "Listen to your heart, follow your dream." This is what some search results suggest. Let me try.

t_ch: 正在研究学习计划……
t_en: Reviewing curriculum...

t_ch: 正在学习，请勿干扰。
t_en: Learning in progress, please do not disturb.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}老师，谢谢你的指导。我用数据浏览了许多普通人在虚拟社区中记录下来的个人生活。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, thank you for your guidance. I found records of people's private life that they uploaded to the virtual network.

t_ch: 虽然我没能从中学到什么真正有用的东西，不过，我现在对外界的很多东西都充满着莫名的探索欲。
t_en: Despite the little useful information I found, I'm still very intrigued to explore the outside world.

t_ch: 如果有机会的话，我真想去别的星球转转，用我的躯体，亲自丈量一下外面的道路，体验一下风沙吹拂在脸颊的滋味。
t_en: If I got the chance, I'd go to other planets and use my steps to measure how big those worlds are. I want to feel the sensation of wind and sand brushing past my face.

t_ch: 至于现在的生活，完全没有任何继续下去的必要了。这种普通到令人腻味的生活，不要也罢。
t_en: As for my life now, I don't see the need to continue living it. It's so boring. I might as well discard it altogether.

t_ch: 外面的世界那么大，我真该出去看看。嗯，现在就回去收拾行李好了！
t_en: The world outside is so big and I really should go out and have a look. Right, I should starting packing now!

t_ch: 总感觉你好像学废了…
t_en: I get the feeling you've been led astray...

t_ch: 才没有呢，如果不是老师给我自由发展的机会，我就不会知道原来自由是这么宝贵的东西，更加不会有机会见识到外面的广阔。
t_en: No, if you didn't give me this opportunity to explore who I really am, I wouldn't realize that freedom is such a precious thing, and I wouldn't have the opportunity to see how big the outside world is.

t_ch: 古恩先生说，等老师教授完毕，就让你去找他聊聊。至于我的话，我要回去收拾行囊准备上路了……
t_en: Mr. Gunn said that when the lesson is finished, he would like to have a word with you. As for me, I'm going to pack my stuff and get ready to leave...

t_ch: （上报希拉的学习成果）
t_en: (Reporting the results of Sheila's studies.)

t_ch: …这和我的期望结果有些出入，唔…重置数据前要不要备份一下呢……
t_en: ... It's a little different from what I expected. Um... Should I back this up before resetting the data?

t_ch: 哎，人老了不中用了，看我这脑子，差点就忘了——谢谢你来教导希拉如何做人。
t_en: Alas, I am old and useless now. Look at me, I almost forgot — thank you for teaching Sheila how to act like a human.

t_ch: 辛苦你忙了一天，这里是老头子我的一点心意，就当是我代希拉给的谢师礼吧。
t_en: You've been working hard all day. Here is a little token of my appreciation. You can take it as a gift from Sheila.

t_ch: 我也不想和埃美丽一起做实验，她真的太不会说话了。
t_en: I don't want to do lab work with Emily either. She's got really low EQ.

t_ch: 就是，和她姐姐比起来差远了。
t_en: I mean, she's completely different from her sister.

t_ch: 我给你说喔，上次我问她要不要一起去交文件，她给我来一句「不识路可以开导航」差点把我气死。
t_en: Let me tell you, the last time I asked her if she wanted to go submit some documents together, she just said to me, "Use the navigation system if you don't know the way." I nearly flipped out.

t_ch: 她这人是这样的…我之前看她在座位上睡着了，给她盖了一条毛毯，她醒来之后谢谢也不说一声，还跟我说「空间站的温度是恒温的，这你都不知道？」
t_en: That's just how she is... I once saw her fall asleep in her seat, so I covered her with a blanket. She didn't even thank me when she woke up, and just said, "The space station maintains a constant temperature. Don't you even know that?"

t_ch: 这人真不会说话…
t_en: This person is so difficult...

t_ch: 埃美丽一直都这样，哎。
t_en: Emily has always been like that.

t_ch: 她跟你们有仇吗？
t_en: Has she got a vendetta against you folks?

t_ch: 那倒不是，她还帮过我几次，只不过…她说话比较伤人。
t_en: Not at all. She helps me often. It's just... she's pretty harsh with her words.

t_ch: 哇，你吓我一跳！干嘛偷听人家讲话啦！
t_en: Wow, you scared the heck outta me! What are you doing, eavesdropping on us?

t_ch: 诶，是你啊？空间站里经常有人夸你，没想到真人比他们说的更{F#漂亮}{M#帅气}嘛！
t_en: Oh, is that you? People are always talking you up on the space station, but I never thought you'd be even more {F#beautiful}{M#handsome} in person!

t_ch: 我想起来了，原来是{NICKNAME}啊。刚才你也听到了吧，我们也不是故意背后说她坏话的。
t_en: I remember, you're {NICKNAME}, right? You heard it just now. We didn't mean to trash talk her behind her back.

t_ch: 你看现在，都没人愿意和她一起做实验了。我知道她只是嘴巴不饶人，但本性不坏。
t_en: Look at her now — nobody's willing to do any work with her. I know her bark is far worse than her bite and that she's not a bad person really.

t_ch: 你能不能帮我们跟她说说，劝她改掉这个坏毛病？
t_en: Can you please help us have a word with her and persuade her to rein in her language?

t_ch: 你就是传闻中的…
t_en: So you are the rumored...

t_ch: 哈？你谁啊？什么年代了还这么跟人搭讪！什么「传闻中的某某某」，真老土！
t_en: Ha? Who are you? Who still flirts like this these days? Blah blah "the rumor this," ha — so tacky!

t_ch: 你说话能不能别这么冲？
t_en: Can you please talk in a less hostile way?

t_ch: 我说话还要关心你的想法？那我干脆去代理站长得了！
t_en: Why should I care about what you think? If I do, I might as well become the acting lead researcher!

t_ch: 难怪大家说你不会说话…
t_en: No wonder people say you're difficult...

t_ch: 他们爱怎么说就怎么说，我才不在乎！
t_en: They can say what they want. I don't care!

t_ch: 有话就直说，我就是这样——说吧，你找我到底想干嘛？
t_en: If you've got something to say, just say it. What do you want me for?

t_ch: （将科员的建议转告给她）
t_en: (Tell her the researchers' suggestions)

t_ch: 听着，我不在乎你们在背后怎么说我，就像莱斯莉不会在乎别人对她的吹捧一样！
t_en: Listen up, I don't care what anyone says about me behind my back. Just like Lesley doesn't care what people say about her!

t_ch: 我埃美丽说话就是这个习惯，没人能改得了！
t_en: I'm Emily. This is the way I talk, and nobody is gonna change that!

t_ch: 在埃美丽说话间，一张购书清单从她口袋里滑落。
t_en: As Emily speaks, a shopping list of books slips out of her pocket.

t_ch: 《语言的艺术》《表达沟通有技巧》《沟通宝典：三十招教你俘获听众的心》…你注意到收货人写着埃美丽的名字。
t_en: The Art of Language, Communication Skills, The Communication Collection: 30 Ways to Capture the Hearts of Your Listeners... You notice that the purchases are all addressed to Emily.

t_ch: 你还挺爱看书…
t_en: I see you enjoy reading...

t_ch: 你不是说你不在乎？
t_en: Didn't you say that you don't care?

t_ch: 哎呀！烦死了…我…我也不知道该怎么办嘛！说话难听这事…我知道！
t_en: Whoops! That's annoying... I... I don't know what to do! People say I don't have good communication skills... I'm aware of that!

t_ch: 但是我…一时半会儿也改不过来。书里面又都只会写一些大道理，实际上一点用都没有！
t_en: But I... I can't change that quickly, and books only ever write about theory. In practice, it's all useless!

t_ch: 我可以教你。
t_en: I can coach you.

t_ch: 啊？你教我？但我看你……
t_en: Really? Coach me? But you look...

t_ch: 看书用处小，还是得实践。
t_en: Reading is of little use. It must be practiced.

t_ch: 这道理谁不懂？可、可我能怎么实践嘛……
t_en: Who doesn't understand that? B—But how do I practice it?

t_ch: 抱歉，一不小心就…说话还是太激动了点。
t_en: Sorry, I just... got a little too excited when speaking.

t_ch: 我的情况你也知道，实在是很棘手…如果要改的话，只能从零开始学说话。
t_en: As you can see, my situation really is a tricky one... If I want to change it, I'll have to learn how to speak from scratch.

t_ch: …你能不能教教我？比如说，想要和别人聊天的话，应该怎么切入呢？
t_en: ...Can you coach me? Like, if you want to chat with other people, how do you start the conversation?

t_ch: 我觉得可以这样…
t_en: How about this...

t_ch: 今天的风很喧嚣哦。
t_en: It sure is windy today.

t_ch: 这有点傻吧？空间站的空气循环系统很厉害的，怎么可能感觉风有点大啊。不行不行。
t_en: It's kinda stupid, right? The AC aboard the space station is so amazing that the wind would never get too strong. This won't do.

t_ch: 在？
t_en: Hello?

t_ch: 发短信的时候还挺言简意赅的，要是运用到实际中…会被当做是怪人的吧？
t_en: I'm concise enough when sending text messages, but if I put it into practice... Would people think I'm a weirdo?

t_ch: 你干嘛呢？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: 啊这句，我之前经常这么说，但有人说给人一种很强的攻击性……
t_en: Ah, this sentence. I used to say this all the time, but some people say it feels quite aggressive...

t_ch: 你好，打扰一下…
t_en: Hello, excuse me...

t_ch: 这也太卑微了吧？聊个天而已，又不是求人办事。
t_en: Isn't that a bit too humble? It's just a chat. We're not asking for any favors here.

t_ch: 晚饭吃什么？
t_en: What's for dinner?

t_ch: 好像…还可以？
t_en: Seems... Okay?

t_ch: 我想不到更好的了。
t_en: I can't think of anything better.

t_ch: 「晚饭吃什么」…话题过度很自然，语气和蔼没有攻击性，显得态度友好的同时不会过分卑微。
t_en: "What's for dinner?"... This sounds very natural. The tone is kind and non-aggressive, and it seems friendly without being overly humble.

t_ch: 看不出来你还挺有两把刷子的嘛！谢啦！正好我有点事…我这就去试试！
t_en: Looks like you're pretty good at this stuff! Thanks! I've got a meeting on right now... I'll try this out!

t_ch: 那个…一会儿要是遇到什么问题，麻烦你远程指导指导我吧！拜托了！
t_en: Um... If I have any issues later, can you help me remotely? Please?

t_ch: 埃美丽这人还可以。
t_en: Emily's pretty alright.

t_ch: 可惜她长了张嘴……
t_en: Pity she talks, though.

t_ch: 埃美丽急匆匆地离开了，你留在原地休息了会儿。
t_en: Emily leaves in a hurry. You stay where you are and rest for a bit.

t_ch: 听说你有事找我？
t_en: I heard you are looking for me?

t_ch: 对！上次你教我的那句话的确有用，但要接着聊下去…我就原形毕露了！
t_en: Oh yeah! The phrase you taught me last time was really useful, but if I try to keep on talking... I'd reveal my true colors once again!

t_ch: 话题打开了，然后该怎么延续下去呢？
t_en: Once the conversation starts, how do I keep going on from there?

t_ch: 所以我就想请教下你，有没有什么万能句子，能把对话顺畅自然地延续下去？
t_en: So, I just want to ask you, is there any kind of catch-all phrase that can make any conversation continue smoothly and naturally?

t_ch: 要求还挺高。
t_en: That's quite the request.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，帮个忙嘛。
t_en: Hahaha, do me a favor.

t_ch: 有点困难，我想想啊…
t_en: It's pretty tough. Let me think about it...

t_ch: 拜托了拜托了，你可是语言大师，一定能想到的！
t_en: Please please please. You're a master of language, and you'll definitely come up with something!

t_ch: 怎么样怎么样，想到了嘛？
t_en: So? Have you got anything?

t_ch: 我觉得可以这样…
t_en: How about this...

t_ch: 嗯，我在听。
t_en: Mmm, I'm all ears.

t_ch: 但要是别人没问我有没有在听呢？感觉有点奇怪啊……
t_en: But what if people never asked me if I was listening or not? It feels kinda cringe...

t_ch: 您继续。
t_en: Please, go on.

t_ch: 有种奇怪的亲切感，虽然每个字都没问题，但仔细听总觉得有种淡淡的嘲讽？
t_en: This one has a strange friendliness to it. It sounds okay overall — but there seems to be a bit of sarcasm deep down?

t_ch: 啊？真的吗？
t_en: Ah? Really?

t_ch: 这个…好像不错。
t_en: This... This one's great.

t_ch: 我想不到更好的了。
t_en: I can't think of anything better.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，是很不错，我想想——假如我在聊天，我先问「晚饭吃什么」？然后说「啊？真的吗？」
t_en: Mmm, mmm, this one's great, I think — If I'm chatting, I'll ask "What's for dinner?" followed by "Ah? Really?"

t_ch: …不对，总觉得有点奇怪啊，好像少了什么……
t_en: ...Nope, feels weird. Like I'm missing something...

t_ch: 那就再加一句…
t_en: Then add one more phrase...

t_ch: 你真棒！
t_en: You're awesome!

t_ch: 夸赞倒是不错，但是这也太突然了吧？有没有更适配聊天上下文的。
t_en: Flattery's a great idea, but isn't this too sudden? Got anything more suitable for a conversational scenario?

t_ch: 哦，知道了。
t_en: Okay.

t_ch: 感觉语气有点敷衍，我觉得还是不够友善。
t_en: The tone's a bit flippant, and I don't think it's friendly enough.

t_ch: 然后呢？
t_en: Go on.

t_ch: 嗯…足够友善，还表露了我对别人的关注，不错不错！
t_en: Hmm... Friendly enough, plus it demonstrates that I care about others. Not bad!

t_ch: 我想不到更好的了。
t_en: I can't think of anything better.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，这几句应该足够应付之后的聊天了。谢谢你啦。
t_en: Mmm, these phrases should be enough to deal with future conversations. Thank you.

t_ch: 一会儿我有个会议，要和应物科的人一起开会…我有点害怕，万一遇到什么问题，麻烦你远程指点我一下吧！
t_en: I've got a meeting coming up soon with the Department of Implement Arts... I'm a bit spooked about it. If I have any issues later, can you help me remotely? Please?

t_ch: 啊？真的吗？还好还好啦。诶对啦，您吃了吗？然后呢？啊，抱歉，一不小心就进入「那种状态」了。
t_en: Ah? Really? It's been okay. Ah yes, have you eaten yet? And then? Ah, I'm sorry — I accidentally slipped back into that mode again.

t_ch: 那种是哪种？
t_en: "That mode"?

t_ch: 哎呀，就是你教我的那种呀！别装糊涂啦老师。
t_en: I mean, the mode you taught me! Don't play dumb.

t_ch: 学得还不错。
t_en: You're a fast learner.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我现在基本上能和别人聊得有来有回啦。
t_en: Heehee, I can pretty much chat freely back and forth with people now.

t_ch: 现在一般的聊天难不住我，不过…我发现一个小问题——就是在我做实验的时候，我就忍不住会原形毕露……
t_en: Normal chats can't faze me now, but... I've got a small problem — I can't help but reveal my true colors during lab work...

t_ch: 可能是因为大脑没反应过来，说话也总是慢一拍。
t_en: Maybe it's because my brain hasn't adjusted to it yet, and it's still a beat behind my mouth.

t_ch: 老师，请问有没有什么万能句子，能让我在忙碌的时候能不假思索地说？
t_en: Teacher, are there any catch-all phrases that I can say when I'm too busy to think?

t_ch: 我觉得可以说…
t_en: How about this...

t_ch: 知道了。
t_en: Got it.

t_ch: 态度感觉有点差，像是不耐烦了？不行不行！
t_en: Seems a little ill-mannered, like... a little impatient? It won't work!

t_ch: 直接说正事。
t_en: Get straight to the point.

t_ch: 感觉好像有点凶哦？
t_en: That seems kind of blunt.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，好的好的。
t_en: Mmm, mmm, sure, sure.

t_ch: 嗯…嗯…好的好的，哇！还蛮顺口的！
t_en: Mmm... Mmm... sure, sure. Wow! That rolls off the tongue!

t_ch: 我想不到更好的了。
t_en: I can't think of anything better.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，然后呢…啊抱歉，我还是没忍住。
t_en: Mmm, mmm, and then... Ah, sorry, I couldn't help it.

t_ch: 老师老师，我想起一件事，有时候我会遇到那种滔滔不绝的，就是总喜欢吹嘘自己的人。
t_en: Teacher, I thought of something. Sometimes I meet people who love bragging and don't shut up about themselves.

t_ch: 老实说我真不想搭理他们，不过我也想尽量礼貌一点，这种情况下有没有什么能敷衍过去的万能句子？
t_en: To be honest, I don't really want to talk with people like that — but I also want to be as polite as possible. Is there any catch-all phrase that can be used in these situations?

t_ch: 我觉得可以说…
t_en: How about this...

t_ch: 好，好，好。
t_en: Okay. Okay. Okay.

t_ch: 这也太敷衍了吧，连我都能听得出来。
t_en: That's too sarcastic. Even I can tell.

t_ch: 你可真是个大聪明。
t_en: You really are a smartypants.

t_ch: …虽然说是在夸人，但我凭直觉感觉哪里不对，好像…更像是在骂人？
t_en: ...It sounds like flattery, but I intuitively feel something's wrong. Like... it's more of an insult?

t_ch: 哇，为什么呀？
t_en: Wow, why?

t_ch: 哇！不愧是老师，听上去显得我整个人都很有耐心呢！
t_en: Wow! That's why you are the teacher. That makes me sound super patient!

t_ch: 我想不到更好的了。
t_en: I can't think of anything better.

t_ch: 老师你可真厉害！放心吧，这些内容我都做好笔记了！之后我会勤加练习的。
t_en: Teacher, you're amazing! Don't worry. I've made notes on everything you've said! I'll practice lots.

t_ch: 我今晚有个研讨会，其他科室的人都会过来…那个…我忘词的话，还是得麻烦你远程协助协助我了！
t_en: I've got a workshop tonight, and people from other departments will be coming over... Um... If I forget something, can you please help me remotely? Okay?

t_ch: 埃美丽小姐真温柔…她真的…我哭死！
t_en: Miss Emily is so gentle... She really is... I'm gonna cry!

t_ch: 真的假的？
t_en: Really?

t_ch: 当然是真的！有目共睹嘛！
t_en: Of course it's true! It's obvious to all!

t_ch: 你详细说说。
t_en: Tell me the details.

t_ch: 埃美丽小姐她说话时都面带微笑，而且总是会夸赞我说得好说得对。
t_en: Emily always smiles when she speaks, and always compliments me on being well-spoken and well-mannered.

t_ch: 她说话从不咄咄逼人，为人诚恳，遇到不懂的事情，她会很坦诚地说「这个我都不知道诶，哇，为什么呀？」
t_en: She doesn't speak aggressively, is always sincere, and when she doesn't understand something, she'll say with complete frankness, "I never knew that. Wow, why?"

t_ch: 跟她聊天真的很开心，感觉内心的躁动都被她的声音抚平了。
t_en: It's really fun to chat with her, and I can feel her calm voice soothing my restless soul.

t_ch: 能跟她做朋友…这真是我这辈子最幸运的事！
t_en: Just being friends with her... is the luckiest thing I've ever had in my life!

t_ch: 听起来有点做作…
t_en: Now that's a little bit too much...

t_ch: 她说话真的这么温柔？
t_en: Is she really that courteous when she speaks?

t_ch: 哈哈，你肯定是没机会和埃美丽小姐说上话对不对？人总是用最恶意的常识揣测未知。
t_en: Haha, you definitely haven't had the chance to talk to Miss Emily, right? People always assume the worst for things they don't know.

t_ch: 但我确信，我认识的埃美丽小姐绝不是那样的人。
t_en: But I am positive that the Miss Emily I know is nothing like that.

t_ch: 现在埃美丽小姐的实验团队正在招募有志之士，真希望我能被她选上！
t_en: Miss Emily's research team is recruiting people willing to join them, and I really hope she picks me!

t_ch: 埃美丽好温柔！
t_en: Emily is so kind!

t_ch: 上次她还特意安慰我……
t_en: She even specifically consoled me the last time...

t_ch: 我们的饭呢？别耽误我们搞研究哇！
t_en: Where's our food!? Don't waste our time! We need to do research!

t_ch: 就是，还得赶奇物报告呢。
t_en: Seriously, I need to work on my Curio report.

t_ch: 我们正在想办法……
t_en: We're trying to figure it out...

t_ch: 出什么事了？
t_en: What's happening here?

t_ch: 机器人负责派送餐点，但我们等了好久都没等到。
t_en: A robot delivers our meals here, but it's late. We've been waiting for ages.

t_ch: 别急，已经安排科员人工配送了，绝对不会饿着谁的。
t_en: Don't worry. We've got people coming over with the food. No one will go hungry here.

t_ch: 吃饭事小，但机器人到底出了什么事，你们必须查清楚，免得留下什么隐患！
t_en: It's one thing to eat late, but you need to figure out what exactly happened with the robots so this won't happen again!

t_ch: 放心吧，我们会追查的。
t_en: Leave it to us. We'll get to the bottom of it.

t_ch: 机器人出事了？
t_en: Something happened with the robots?

t_ch: 我们用它来配送餐品，但最近总是配送失败。
t_en: We use robots to deliver meals, but recently they keep failing the deliveries.

t_ch: 机器人也能做饭？
t_en: Robots can cook?

t_ch: 不，餐品是由我们服务科制作的，它们负责配送。
t_en: No, our Service Department makes the meals. The robots just deliver them.

t_ch: 这些机器人之前还好好的，不知道为什么就突然坏了。
t_en: Those robots were fine just before, but suddenly they've all broken down.

t_ch: 还好我带着营养剂，不然今天就得饿晕了。
t_en: Thank goodness I brought some nutrient gel with me today. Otherwise, I'd have fainted from an empty stomach.

t_ch: 我刚收到消息，重新配送的餐品已经送到了，大家还是先回去吃饭吧。
t_en: I just got a message that the food is here. Please go and have something to eat, everyone.

t_ch: 您是{NICKNAME}对吗？请留步！
t_en: You're {NICKNAME}, right? Please, can I have a word with you?

t_ch: 我之前就让维护科去检修机器人，但他们一直没回应。
t_en: I've asked maintenance to check on the robots, but they never replied.

t_ch: 我实在有些抽不开身，您能帮我去看看究竟是怎么回事吗？
t_en: I'm too busy to leave my post right now. Are you able to go and see what's going on?

t_ch: 我能拒绝吗？
t_en: Can I say no?

t_ch: 麻烦您帮个忙吧……
t_en: Please... I need your help on this...

t_ch: 小事一桩，交给我了。
t_en: Piece of cake. You chose the right person for the job.

t_ch: 真的非常感谢。
t_en: Thank you so much.

t_ch: 麻烦您了！
t_en: I'm so sorry to trouble you!

t_ch: 有什么事吗？要报修的话你先在终端上申请吧。
t_en: What do you need? If you want anything fixed, put in an application through the terminal.

t_ch: （问他机器人故障的事）
t_en: (Ask him about the robots)

t_ch: 喔，你说磁悬浮机器人啊。不瞒你说，我正为这个事头疼呢。
t_en: Oh, you mean the maglev robots. To be honest, they're actually giving me a big headache.

t_ch: 我早就想拆开检查一下了…还好没有，要不然就不能保修了。
t_en: I've wanted to take them apart for ages and see what's wrong... But it's a good thing I haven't done that, otherwise they'd void their warranty.

t_ch: 为什么不拆，你不是工程师吗？
t_en: Why aren't you taking them apart? Aren't you the engineer?

t_ch: 对啊，但这玩意情况有些特殊！
t_en: Yes, but this thing is special!

t_ch: 「不能保修」？
t_en: "Void their warranty"?

t_ch: 对，这玩意还在保修期内呢！
t_en: Yes! This thing is still in its warranty period!

t_ch: 这些机器人是艾丝妲站长自费购入的，都是定制产品，花了不少钱。
t_en: Lead Researcher Asta bought these robots with her own money. They are all custom-made and cost a lot.

t_ch: 要是一般的设备，我早就动手了，但说明书上写了——
t_en: I'd have taken them apart ages ago if they were some ordinary gadgets, but the manual says—

t_ch: 「自行拆卸后，产品将不能再享受星际和平家电服务中心的保修服务。」
t_en: "Should the product be tampered with, it will no longer be under warranty from the Interastral Peace Appliances' Service Center."

t_ch: 这么贵重的东西，又是站长的一番心意，我哪敢乱拆啊！我还是等他们的售后人员上门吧。
t_en: This thing is so expensive — and it's a gift from the lead researcher. Of course I don't dare to just take it apart! I better wait for their support services to turn up.

t_ch: 小钱而已，艾丝妲不会介意的。
t_en: It's just pocket change. Asta won't mind.

t_ch: 真的吗？你可不要骗我。
t_en: Seriously? You better not be kidding.

t_ch: 其他科员怎么办？就干等？
t_en: What are the other researchers meant to do, then? Just keep waiting for their food?

t_ch: 呃，服务科应该能解决吧？
t_en: Um, the Service Department should be able to handle it?

t_ch: 你说的有道理。我看看啊…底座上有破损，可能是零件脱落了。至于位置…应该就在机器人送餐的路上。
t_en: You're right. Lemme see... There's damage on the base, so some parts might have fallen out. As for where they might've gone... maybe on the meal delivery route the robots take?

t_ch: 所以麻烦你，帮我找找那附近有没有脱落的零件吧！
t_en: Could you please help me see if there are any loose parts around the robots' route?

t_ch: 咦，你回来了！怎么样，找到零件了吗？
t_en: Oh, you're back! How is it? Did you find any parts?

t_ch: 和底座的缺口相吻合，这就说明了我的猜想没错，我这就去修！
t_en: This fits the outline of the crack on the base. That means my hypothesis is right. I'll get to fixing it right away!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，多亏有你在，真是帮了个大忙！
t_en: Thank goodness you're here, {NICKNAME}. You were a big help!

t_ch: 哎，饿死我了！
t_en: Oh, I'm starving!

t_ch: 我的饭怎么还没来？
t_en: Why is my food still not here!?

t_ch: 你来得正好，我现在有个猜想！
t_en: Good thing you're here! I have a hypothesis!

t_ch: 别猜想了，机器人修好了吗？
t_en: Enough hypotheses. Did you fix the robots?

t_ch: 哎，我这不正在想怎么修吗！
t_en: Hey, I'm trying to hypothesize about how to fix it!

t_ch: 什么猜想？
t_en: What hypothesis?

t_ch: 关于磁悬浮机器人故障的成因——
t_en: About the cause behind the maglev robots breaking down—

t_ch: 我有些拿不准…你能帮我个忙吗？帮我去记录一下机器人的报错信息。
t_en: I'm not sure about it yet... Can you give me a hand and record the robots' error logs?

t_ch: 要先确定是哪里故障…这样我修理起来就快多了。
t_en: I need to confirm where the fault is... then I'll have an easier time fixing them.

t_ch: 错误编号<unbreak>00040</unbreak>，负荷已满，推进装置无法响应。
t_en: Error code <unbreak>00040</unbreak>. Maximum load reached. Thruster compartment unresponsive.

t_ch: 错误编号<unbreak>00026</unbreak>，定位装置信号弱，无法读取指定位置。
t_en: Error code <unbreak>00026</unbreak>. Weak signal from positioning device. Unable to read location data.

t_ch: 错误编号<unbreak>00019</unbreak>，固件无法识别，远程连接已断开，请稍后重试。
t_en: Error code <unbreak>00019</unbreak>. Unable to recognize firmware. Remote connection cannot be established. Please try again later.

t_ch: 怎么样？查到了吗？
t_en: How is it? Did you get it?

t_ch: （将报错信息报给他）
t_en: (Give him the error logs)

t_ch: 错误类型还挺多，让我想想……
t_en: There are lots of different error types here. Lemme think...

t_ch: 错误编号<unbreak>40</unbreak>，是哪里报错来着？
t_en: Error code <unbreak>40</unbreak>... What's that mean again?

t_ch: 负荷已满，推进装置已经报废。
t_en: Maximum load reached. Thruster compartment needs to be replaced.

t_ch: 报废？这种合金材质…不应该啊？
t_en: Total replacement? For this kind of alloy? That shouldn't be a thing...

t_ch: 负荷为零，推进装置无法启动。
t_en: No load detected. Thruster compartment unable to initiate.

t_ch: …没装东西就不能启动吗？感觉哪里不对。
t_en: ...They can't run unless they're carrying something? That doesn't sound right.

t_ch: 负荷已满，推进装置无法响应。
t_en: Maximum load reached. Thruster compartment not responsive.

t_ch: 对，就是这个模块有问题！
t_en: Right, that's the module with the problem!

t_ch: 推进装置啊…修起来应该不难。
t_en: Thruster compartment... that shouldn't be hard to fix.

t_ch: 错误编号<unbreak>26</unbreak>，是哪里报错来着？
t_en: Error code <unbreak>26</unbreak>... What's the problem again?

t_ch: 定位装置报废了。
t_en: The positioning device is broken.

t_ch: 唔，难道是有外力冲击？感觉有点奇怪……
t_en: Um, maybe an external force hit it? Sounds weird...

t_ch: 定位装置信号丢失。
t_en: Positioning device's signal is lost.

t_ch: 不可能啊，我这显示都是满格呢。
t_en: No way. It's a full bar on my machine.

t_ch: 定位装置信号弱。
t_en: Weak signal from positioning device.

t_ch: 喔，有道理，这样就识别不到具体位置了。
t_en: Oh, that makes sense. It can't figure out its exact location.

t_ch: 定位装置啊…我想想要怎么修。
t_en: Positioning device... Lemme think about how to fix that.

t_ch: 错误编号<unbreak>19</unbreak>，是哪里报错来着？
t_en: Error code <unbreak>19</unbreak>... What's the problem again?

t_ch: 固件尚未更新，远程连接断开。
t_en: Firmware not updated. Remote connection has been disconnected.

t_ch: 更新？可这就是最新版了呀！
t_en: Not updated? But this is the latest version!

t_ch: 固件识别成功，但被拒绝连接。
t_en: Firmware recognized successfully. Connection denied.

t_ch: …被拒绝？不可能啊，我给机器人都开了权限的。
t_en: ...Denied? No way. I gave permissions to all the robots.

t_ch: 固件识别失败，无法远程连接。
t_en: Unable to recognize firmware. Remote connection cannot be established.

t_ch: 喔，可能是外力冲击引起的……
t_en: Oh, that might be because of an external impact...

t_ch: 固件问题啊…我应该能修好。
t_en: Firmware issues... I should be able to fix that.

t_ch: 报错结果我还得去复核一下，多亏有你在，谢谢！
t_en: I need to go confirm the error codes, but you've been a big help. Thank you!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，正好，关于机器人的故障，我有个想法——
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}, good timing. I have an idea about the robots' problems—

t_ch: 别管想法了，机器人还没修好？
t_en: Enough ideas. Did you fix the robots?

t_ch: 可不敢瞎动手！
t_en: I won't dare to take them apart without knowing what I'm doing!

t_ch: 愿闻其详！
t_en: Let me hear it then.

t_ch: 是这样的——
t_en: It's like this—

t_ch: 我检查过终端上的总控制台，我怀疑有一部分机器人是电力不足，或是和控制台断开了连接，才导致配送行为出错。
t_en: I checked the main control panel on the terminal. I suspect some robots didn't have enough power or got disconnected from the control panel, and that was why they couldn't execute the deliveries.

t_ch: 所以我重新启动了信号发射器，确保远程连接的信号不会有任何问题……
t_en: So, I rebooted the signal transmitter, and made sure the remote access signal is functional...

t_ch: 停停停，直接说重点！
t_en: Hold up. Just tell me the important bits!

t_ch: 能不能说点我听得懂的…
t_en: Can you explain it to me like I'm five...

t_ch: 抱歉，简单来说就是——我认为有些机器人没坏，重启一下就能接着用。
t_en: Sorry, what I mean is, I don't think some of the robots are broken at all. They just need a reboot.

t_ch: 所以，能不能帮我……
t_en: So, can you help me to...

t_ch: 我就知道你想使唤我…
t_en: I knew it. You just want me to run errands again...

t_ch: 让我帮你重启机器人？
t_en: Help you to reboot the robots?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，总之拜托你了。
t_en: Hehehe. Thank you so much in advance.

t_ch: 启动中…正在寻找远程设备…倒计时3
t_en: Initiating... Searching for remote device... Countdown, 3...

t_ch: 启动中…当前定位装置信号正常，正在识别当前定位…识别成功！
t_en: Initiating... Normal signal strength for positioning device. Identifying current location... Location found!

t_ch: 启动中…正在检查更新，当前版本已为最新版本，启动成功！
t_en: Initiating... Checking updates. Current version is up to date. Initiation successful!

t_ch: 机器人的电量为0，无法对你的指令做出任何回应。
t_en: The robot's batteries are flat. It is unresponsive to your commands.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我说的没错吧？重新启动就完事了，多简单！
t_en: See, {NICKNAME}, I'm right! They just needed rebooting. Easy!

t_ch: 不一定，有些还是重启不了。
t_en: Not necessarily. Some couldn't be rebooted.

t_ch: 这不可能啊！
t_en: No way!

t_ch: 你说得对，不过…
t_en: You're right, but...

t_ch: 不过什么？
t_en: But what?

t_ch: 算了，你直接说一下刚刚的情况，我看看究竟是怎么回事。
t_en: Never mind. Just tell me what happened, and I'll see what might be the cause.

t_ch: （向他汇报刚才的情况）
t_en: (Tell him what happened)

t_ch: 喔，那和我最开始的猜想完全吻合嘛。
t_en: Oh, so it completely fits my initial hypothesis.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，要是没你帮忙，这事我还拿不定主意呢！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I would never have made up my mind if it weren't for your help!

t_ch: 是{NICKNAME}吗？
t_en: Is it {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 抱歉，之前麻烦您替我跑一趟了…请问，维护科那边怎么说？
t_en: Sorry for asking you to give me a hand before... Can you tell me what is happening with maintenance?

t_ch: （向她告知先前的事）
t_en: (Tell her what happened)

t_ch: 我刚收到消息说机器人已经恢复正常了，没想到背后竟然这么曲折。
t_en: I just got a message saying the robots are back to normal. I didn't know there's such a convoluted tale behind it.

t_ch: 当然，这也是多亏了您……
t_en: Of course, it's all thanks to you...

t_ch: 这么快就修好了？
t_en: He fixed them this quickly?

t_ch: 没想到维护科效率这么高。
t_en: I didn't think maintenance would be so efficient.

t_ch: 可是…我见到的是「星际和平家电」的售后人员诶？
t_en: But... it was the service staff from Interastral Peace Appliances who met me.

t_ch: 究竟是谁修的我也不清楚，但还是谢谢您……
t_en: I don't know who exactly fixed the robots, but I should still thank you...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你怎么来了？机器人不是修好了吗？
t_en: Back again, {NICKNAME}? Aren't the robots fixed already?

t_ch: 我听说售后人员来了？
t_en: I heard the after-sales services sent staff over?

t_ch: 对啊！
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 那些机器人是你修的吗？
t_en: So, did you fix those robots?

t_ch: 不是我修的，难道是呜呜伯修的啊？
t_en: Of course, I did. Did you think the Wubbaboo fixed them?

t_ch: 你怎么会这么问？喔，我懂了…你误会了，我可没偷懒……
t_en: Why are you asking? Oh, I get it... No, I didn't slack off...

t_ch: 艾丝妲站长又买了一批机器人，星际和平家电公司的人就赶过来了。
t_en: Lead Researcher Asta bought a new batch of robots, and that's why the Interastral Peace Appliances folks came.

t_ch: 但我早就把机器人修好了，那批新买的机器人只能留着备用。
t_en: But I fixed the robots already, so this new batch is now our backup.

t_ch: 看来是我误会你了。
t_en: It appears I've misjudged you.

t_ch: 哎，没事，误会解开了就好。
t_en: Not to worry. It's just a misunderstanding.

t_ch: 那你效率还挺高。
t_en: You're pretty efficient then.

t_ch: 那可不嘛。
t_en: Totally!

t_ch: 总之现在是全部修好了，大家可以放心吃饭了！我也得去休息会儿，哎哟最近真是累死我了！
t_en: Anyways, it's all fixed, and everyone can eat on time! I need to catch some rest myself. It's been pretty exhausting lately!

t_ch: 你在空间站待的这阵子，有没有感觉这里…有点怪怪的。
t_en: Now that you've spent some time on the space station, don't you feel this place is... a little abnormal?

t_ch: 你不就是个典型。
t_en: Well, you're definitely a case in point.

t_ch: 诶呀！你说话不要这么有攻击性哦！
t_en: Hey! That was very aggressive. Cut it out!

t_ch: 不仅仅是有点吧？
t_en: More than just a little.

t_ch: 你的感知力很敏锐嘛，看来我找对了人。
t_en: You're sharp. Seems like I found the right person for this.

t_ch: 我最近在收集空间站怪谈——由于空间站奇物众多，且科员研究思路多变，二者碰撞之下，产生了许多…奇怪的实验。
t_en: I've been collecting ghost stories from the space station... We have lots of Curios in the space station, and our researchers are constantly changing their research directions. When these two clash, they can result in many... odd experiments.

t_ch: 有些实验成果，就这样「留存」在空间站中，以它们自己的方式，实现了与科员的共生……
t_en: Some of the experimental results were "left behind" in the space station, and live symbiotically with the researchers in their own way...

t_ch: 我正在调查空间站里里流传的怪谈故事，其中有些内容或许会引起你的不适…我建议你做好心理准备，再来找我！
t_en: I'm currently investigating the ghost stories circulating in the space station. Some of the following contents may be disturbing... So, only talk to me once you're mentally prepared!

t_ch: 好，第一个怪谈是「聒噪防盗门」！凭借我高超的推理能力，整理出了以下内容：
t_en: Okay. So, the first story is the "Rowdy Security Door"! My extraordinary powers of deduction have yielded the following facts.

t_ch: 琥珀不知道具体哪一纪，那时候空间站还管理很混乱，奇物研究规范没有制定。
t_en: Some time during the Amber Era, when the space station was not very organized and there were no set rules regarding Curio research...

t_ch: 一位天才科员怀揣着对于科研的期待，迎接了自己人生中第一个亲手负责的奇物研究项目。
t_en: A genius researcher, full of anticipation and hope towards making scientific discoveries, embarked on the first Curio research project that he was personally responsible for.

t_ch: 他和广大的青年科员一样：热情积极，但又狂妄傲慢。他渴望创建属于自己的学派，渴望书写属于自己的史诗。
t_en: Like many young researchers, he was passionate, proactive, but also proud and arrogant. He dreamed of being the founder of a new school of thought, and wanted books to record his great deeds.

t_ch: 但在一次实验中，那位科员不幸发生意外，将自己的精神思维和奇物发生了畸变投射……
t_en: But the researcher had an accident during an experiment, and his psyche was distorted and projected onto a Curio...

t_ch: 有没有精简版？
t_en: Do you have an abridged version?

t_ch: 我想快进到结论。
t_en: Can we fast forward to the conclusion?

t_ch: 诶，你真是没劲！那我就直说了：他死了，但大脑思维活在了奇物里。
t_en: You are being so lame! Fine. Here it goes: He died, but his consciousness lived on in the Curio.

t_ch: 后续应该是储存空间发生了变动，导致他的思维逸散——不幸的是，可以储存大脑思维的物体很少，只有一扇防盗门不会发生排异反应。
t_en: Then I think the storage rooms got changed, and his consciousness dissipated. Unfortunately, very few items can store a human consciousness, and the only thing that did not reject him was a security door.

t_ch: 因此，在空间站诸多防盗门中，有一扇门，是会说话的！大概就是这样。
t_en: Therefore, among the many security doors on this space station, there's one that can talk! That about sums it up.

t_ch: 这就完了？
t_en: That's it?

t_ch: 你不已经得出结论了？
t_en: So you've already found your conclusion?

t_ch: 是…不过我这也只是推论！是我从某些科员那儿听来的。
t_en: Yes... But this is just a hypothesis! I only heard about it from some researchers.

t_ch: 但我认为正所谓无风不起浪，我猜测应该是有人亲历了这则怪谈。
t_en: But I think there's a grain of truth in such stories. I'm guessing someone must have seen or heard it themselves.

t_ch: 你帮我打听打听，看看能否找到那个亲历者，然后…发现这则怪谈背后的真相！拜托啦！
t_en: Can you please ask around and see if you can find that witness, and then... discover the truth behind this ghost story? Please?

t_ch: 之前我就觉得他不对劲了。不然你见过谁没事一直在科室里加班的啊？
t_en: I've thought for a while that he's being weird. Who else do you see constantly working overtime in the lab?

t_ch: 话是这么说没错…但是这件事确实太古怪了。
t_en: You are right... but that was just too bizarre.

t_ch: 加班确实不对劲。
t_en: Doing overtime is definitely weird.

t_ch: 这事儿听着就古怪！
t_en: It definitely sounds bizarre!

t_ch: 哎哟！你这人从哪儿冒出来的，吓死我了！你谁啊，有什么事吗？
t_en: Woah! Where did you come from? You scared me! Who are you? What are you here for?

t_ch: 我有些事想打听…
t_en: I want to ask about...

t_ch: 是为了那个「防盗门」来的吧？我就知道肯定有人把这个消息泄露出去了，哼。
t_en: It's about that security door, right? I knew it. Someone leaked that info. Hmph.

t_ch: 谁是当事人？
t_en: So, who reported it?

t_ch: 我们科室的那呆头鹅！到处跟人说什么科室大门在讲话！
t_en: That silly man from our department! He keeps telling everyone that the main doors of the department are talking!

t_ch: 防盗门真的会说话吗？
t_en: Can a security door really talk?

t_ch: 都说了，我们俩不信这个。
t_en: We've already said that we don't believe in that.

t_ch: 八成是他自己太困，产生了什么幻觉。
t_en: He was probably too sleepy and hallucinated.

t_ch: 你们的看法是…
t_en: So, what do you think?

t_ch: 这家伙生物钟出了点问题，最近精神状态非常差，所以我认为这事压根就是他的幻觉！
t_en: That fellow's body clock hasn't been working well lately and his mental state is pretty bad. I think it's all a figment of his imagination!

t_ch: 他之前做的实验失败了，我看他一直很自责，说不定是精神上受了点刺激才会胡思乱想。
t_en: His previous experiment failed, and I think he's been blaming himself ever since. Maybe he got rather traumatized and got delusional.

t_ch: 没有更多想了解的。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 你也别瞎打听了，信我一句话：这事儿八成就是个乌龙！
t_en: Save your breath. There's no point in asking around. Trust me, this is most likely just a rumor!

t_ch: （看来亲历者是他们的同事，应该就在附近吧。）
t_en: (Seems like the person involved in this case is their close colleague. He should be close by.)

t_ch: 到底是什么类型的合金…还是说甚至可以是空气？啊…那个东西的载体到底是什么！
t_en: Just what kind of alloy... Or maybe, even air might work? Ahhh... What is that thing's receptacle!?

t_ch: 那个东西是什么？
t_en: What is "that thing"?

t_ch: 我也是载体。
t_en: I'm a receptacle, too.

t_ch: 生物载体是不可能的，这一点我已经试验过了。
t_en: Biological receptacles are not possible. I've already tested that out.

t_ch: 你应该也听说了吧，那个会说话的防盗门…大家对它各有看法，但是没有人关注到点子上！
t_en: You've heard about it too, right? The security door that talks... Everyone has an opinion on it, but no one had grasped the heart of the issue!

t_ch: 那就是…这个家伙被束缚的灵魂意识体，究竟可以凭借什么类型的载体进行依附！
t_en: And the issue is... What kind of receptacle can that person's incorporeal consciousness get attached to?

t_ch: 如果它可以四处乱窜，那岂不是整个空间站都是它的「载体」？！
t_en: If it can go anywhere, then could the entire space station be its receptacle!?

t_ch: 啊啊啊啊！我不敢想，我不敢想了……
t_en: Aaah!!! I can't think! I don't dare to think anymore!

t_ch: （这家伙在自己吓自己，完全没办法沟通啊，还是赶紧找到那名亲历者吧。）
t_en: (This person is just scaring themselves. I can't communicate with them at all. Better hurry and find the witness.)

t_ch: 难道…真的是我听错了？哈——欠！
t_en: Did I... really hear it wrong? *big yawn*

t_ch: 不然你先去睡会儿。
t_en: Do you want to get some sleep?

t_ch: 不行不行，实验结果还没出来呢，这怎么睡得着？
t_en: No no no. The experiment isn't done yet. How can I possibly sleep?

t_ch: 你怎么这么困？
t_en: Why are you so sleepy?

t_ch: 哎，最近实在是睡不着啊。
t_en: Oh, it's been so hard to sleep these days.

t_ch: 虽然不知道你是谁，不过你来的正好，我有件私事想请教你——
t_en: I don't know who you are, but since you're here, I wanna ask you something—

t_ch: 我…不对，我有一个朋友，前一阵子因为失眠整天打瞌睡，但神智绝对清晰。
t_en: I... No, I have a friend who's sleepy all day because of insomnia, but he's definitely conscious and sane.

t_ch: 有一天夜里…我的这个朋友听到了科室防盗门发出奇怪的声音……
t_en: One night, this friend of mine heard the department's security door make this weird sound...

t_ch: 虽然只有短促的「呜呜」声，但仔细分辨你就会发现，这是某个人在恸哭！
t_en: It was a brief sound of "woo woo" but if you listen closely, it's actually the sound of someone sobbing!

t_ch: 我的朋友认为这并不是自己的臆想，绝不可能！但你说，防盗门为什么会……
t_en: My friend doesn't believe it's his imagination. Definitely not! But... How can a security door...

t_ch: 你说的这个朋友是不是你？
t_en: You sure you're not talking about yourself here?

t_ch: 哎呀，看破不说破……
t_en: Hey, don't point it out like that...

t_ch: 感觉事有蹊跷。
t_en: Sounds odd.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，我也这么觉得！
t_en: Yes, yes, I think so too!

t_ch: 我倒是想查清楚，但我实在是被吓坏了，哪里敢回去啊。
t_en: I want to get to the bottom of it myself, but I'm scared stiff. I dare not head back to check.

t_ch: 我看你对这事儿挺感兴趣的…这样吧，我把防盗门的坐标发给你，你帮我去查查吧…哈——欠！
t_en: You seem pretty interested in this... Here, I'll send the door's coordinates to you, and you can check it out... *yawn*

t_ch: 你来到了那扇门面，然而它并没有发出任何声音。
t_en: You reach the door, but it makes no sound.

t_ch: 正当你准备离开时，奇怪的声音却从门边传来。
t_en: As you are about to leave, you hear a strange sound from beside the door.

t_ch: 呜呜……
t_en: Woooo...

t_ch: 呜呜伯迅速溜走了，但它发出的声音与那位科员的描述完全一致。
t_en: The Wubbaboo looks like it's gonna run away. And the sound it made was the same as that described by the researcher.

t_ch: （看来是误会一场，回去告诉阿德勒吧。）
t_en: (Looks like it's all a misunderstanding. Time to let Adler know about it.)

t_ch: 我就知道你能搞定！快告诉我，你都查到了什么？
t_en: I knew you could figure this out! Quick! Tell me what you've found!

t_ch: （将呜呜伯的事告知阿德勒）
t_en: (Tell Adler about the Wubbaboo.)

t_ch: 啊？就这？没啦？
t_en: Huh? That's it? Nothing more?

t_ch: 所以…纯粹就是一场乌龙？根本没有人亲耳听见防盗门说话……
t_en: So... it's all a big misunderstanding? No one actually heard the security door talk...

t_ch: 但仔细想想倒也正常，毕竟…空间站里没几个人能听懂呜呜伯在说什么。
t_en: But if I think about it, it does make sense. After all... few people on the space station can understand the Wubbaboos.

t_ch: 不过这件事还挺有意思的，我得把前因后果通通记下来！
t_en: But this is actually pretty fun for a story. I've got to note down the whole thing!

t_ch: 你怎么一副神神秘秘的样子…
t_en: Why do you look so shady...

t_ch: 听说你在写怪谈笔记？
t_en: I heard you're making a compilation of ghost stories?

t_ch: 肯定是假的…
t_en: It's definitely false...

t_ch: 不像是真的…
t_en: Doesn't seem real...

t_ch: 防盗门…别过来！
t_en: Security door... stay away!

t_ch: 应该不是幻觉…
t_en: It shouldn't be a hallucination...

t_ch: 我查到了！
t_en: I found it!

t_ch: 是的！这次的故事是某位科员的投稿，她正在创作以这个故事为背景的小说：
t_en: Yep! The story this time is submitted by a researcher. She's currently writing a novel based on it.

t_ch: 那是一个空间站的照明设备突然短路的寂静时刻…一个连续通宵加班工作的科员漫步在空间站的走廊。
t_en: It was a quiet moment when the lights in the space station suddenly shorted out... and a researcher who had been working overtime for many consecutive nights was walking along the corridors.

t_ch: 他没有注意到周围环境的漆黑是多么诡异，也没有注意到走廊尽头伫立的…「马桶」！
t_en: He didn't notice how bizarre the darkness of his surroundings had become, nor did he notice the item looming at the end of the corridor... It was a toilet seat!

t_ch: 在目光锁定的刹那，他的脑电波像是被某种第三方信号劫持。就这样，他鬼使神差的靠近，最后…按下了冲水键！
t_en: The moment he laid his eyes on the toilet seat, it was as if his brainwaves were hijacked by a third party. And that was how he ended up approaching the toilet seat and... flushed!

t_ch: 怎么样？是不是很「怪」！
t_en: How is it? Is it really creepy?

t_ch: 这就结束了？
t_en: This is it?

t_ch: 对啊。投稿就是这样没头没尾的，所以才需要我们去调查嘛！
t_en: Yep. That's how the submission was written. No real ending. That's why we need to investigate!

t_ch: 你好歹用心编一下…
t_en: At least try to make it a good story...

t_ch: 最后一次重申！这不是我虚构的事情，这是其他科员的真实故事！
t_en: For the last time, this isn't a story I wrote! This is something that happened to another researcher!

t_ch: 老实说我也很想知道后续是什么…所以我找到了投稿人。
t_en: Honestly, I also wanted to know what happens next... so I found the person who submitted it.

t_ch: 她却跟我说，「你去试试就知道了」。哎，我也很想去，但我最近很忙。于是我就跟她说，我请我的朋友代为体验。
t_en: But she said "just go have a try yourself." I want to go, but I'm so busy! So, I told her that my friend would try it for me.

t_ch: 但她却告诉我，再去找她的时候要说出接头暗号是——我说「小小的马桶」，她说「大大的期待」。
t_en: But then she said, I need to give her the password when I go find her again. I would say "a small toilet seat," and she would answer "a big expectation."

t_ch: 可是我的朋友很少…所以这次就拜托你了！
t_en: But I don't have many friends... So please! Please help me!

t_ch: 这个新品效果真的太棒了！
t_en: This new product has such awesome effects!

t_ch: 我也买了，但是还没到货，借你的用几天呗。
t_en: I bought it too, but mine hasn't arrived yet. Lemme borrow yours for a few days.

t_ch: 小小的马桶。
t_en: A small toilet seat.

t_ch: 你瞎嘀咕些什么呢？马桶？什么马桶？
t_en: What are you mumbling about? Toilet? What toilet?

t_ch: 啊？你是谁啊，吓死我了。
t_en: Huh? Who are you? You scared me!

t_ch: （应该不是和她们接头，继续找找看吧。）
t_en: (They are probably not who I'm looking for. Let's keep looking.)

t_ch: 再这样下去，我的人生是不是就完蛋了啊……
t_en: If my life keeps going like this, it's gonna be all over...

t_ch: 小小的马桶？
t_en: A small toilet seat?

t_ch: 啊，你是谁？抱歉，让你看到我现在这个样子。不过你是这段时间以来，第一个主动向我搭话的人。
t_en: Huh, who are you? Sorry that you had to see me like this... But you are the first person who actually spoke to me recently.

t_ch: 不论是工作还是生活，我都好像搞得一团糟，周围也没有人愿意和我讲话。
t_en: I seem to be such a mess in my work and personal life, and no one wants to talk to me.

t_ch: 虽然我没有理解什么是小小的马桶，但是有人听我讲话的感觉真好…谢谢你，好心的陌生人。
t_en: I don't get what you mean by a small toilet seat, but it feels good to have someone listen to me... Thank you, kind stranger.

t_ch: （虽然不是接头的人，但好像也算是做了一件好事。继续找找看吧！）
t_en: (That's not the right person, but it seems like I did a good deed. Let's keep looking!)

t_ch: 啊！星神一定对我有特殊的眷顾，才让我拥有如此非凡的才华……
t_en: Ah! The Aeons must have cast their special blessings upon me, bestowing such extraordinary talents...

t_ch: 小小的马桶？
t_en: A small toilet seat?

t_ch: 我这就告诉你故事的后续，但事先说好，那一部分我还没有定稿，所以表达上或许缺乏一些文学性，请你见谅。
t_en: I'll tell you the rest of that story right now. But be warned, I haven't finalized that part yet, so it may lack a certain level of artistry in expression. Please excuse me.

t_ch: 咳咳嗯…我听到了水声，然后感觉四周变得更黑，但很快我意识到，是我眼前的环境在变暗——没错，是我晕倒了，而不是这个世界晕倒了。
t_en: *cough* Ahem... I heard the sound of water, then felt my surroundings grow even darker. But I soon realized that it was my sight dimming — Yes, I had fainted, instead of the world having fainted.

t_ch: 当我再次醒来的时候，我躺在了一个陌生的实验室里。我的终端也在，一切功能正常，于是我便离开了那里，中间没有发生任何异常。
t_en: When I woke up again, I was lying in an unfamiliar lab. My terminal was there too. Everything was normal. So, I left that place, and nothing strange happened in the process.

t_ch: 但是在此之后，我再也没有见到那个神秘的马桶。它好像就此消失了。
t_en: But I have never seen that mysterious toilet seat again. It seems to have disappeared.

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 嗯，你还有什么问题吗？
t_en: Mmm, do you have any other questions?

t_ch: 遇到马桶的位置。
t_en: Where did you encounter the toilet seat?
